_meta.description: Structured acceptance criteria for Chargeback Evidence Pack Builder v1. Do not delete entries. Update status field only. UX acceptance criteria added in Phase 3. UX acceptance criteria added in Phase 3.

id.name: Stripe Checkout Integration
id.description: One-time and subscription checkout via Stripe hosted Checkout

id.name: Webhook-Driven Entitlements
id.description: Idempotent webhook handler with signature verification and credit provisioning

id.name: 6-State Access Machine
id.description: Server-side access state derivation: unpaid, one_time_active, subscription_active, subscription_canceling, subscription_past_due, subscription_expired

id.name: Stripe Customer Portal
id.description: Customer portal for payment history, receipts, subscription management

id.name: Billing UI Components
id.description: Pricing cards, checkout button, billing status display, portal button
User can create an account with email and password
steps: Navigate to /signup
Enter valid email address
Enter password meeting minimum requirements (8+ chars)
Click Create Account
Confirm email via verification link
passes: Account created in Supabase Auth
Verification email sent
User redirected to dashboard after verification
No duplicate account can be created with same email
id.notes: Must use Supabase Auth. No OAuth in v1.
ux_acceptance: Signup form renders with email, password, and confirm password fields
All required fields show inline validation errors before form submission
Submit button shows loading state and disables to prevent double-submit
Success state shows email confirmation alert — no auto-navigation
If email already registered: error alert 'An account with this email already exists'
Password mismatch: inline error on confirm password field
Password under 8 chars: inline error 'Password must be at least 8 characters'
Form is accessible — all fields have labels and keyboard navigation works

id.description: User can log in with email and password
steps: Navigate to /login
Enter registered email and password
Click Sign In
passes: User is authenticated and redirected to dashboard
Invalid credentials show error message (not which field is wrong)
Session persists across browser refresh
Brute force protection active (rate limiting)
id.notes: Use Supabase session. Error message must not reveal whether email or password is wrong.
ux_acceptance: Login form renders with email and password fields
Invalid credentials show single alert 'Invalid email or password' — no field-level disclosure
Submit button shows loading state and disables to prevent double-submit
Successful login redirects to dashboard within 2 seconds
Forgot password link is visible and navigates to password reset flow
Empty fields trigger inline validation message before network request
Auth token is stored securely (httpOnly cookie or equivalent) — not in localStorage

id.description: User can reset password via email
steps: Navigate to /login
Click Forgot password
Enter email address
Click Send reset link
Follow link in email
Enter new password
passes: Reset email sent if account exists (silent if not)
Reset link expires after 1 hour
New password accepted and user logged in
id.notes: Silent behavior on non-existent email prevents account enumeration.
ux_acceptance: 'Continue with Google' button renders on both login and signup pages
Clicking opens Google OAuth consent screen
New Google users are created in the system and redirected to onboarding
Returning Google users are redirected to dashboard
OAuth error or cancellation shows toast notification — not blank screen
Button shows loading/spinner state while OAuth flow is in progress
No duplicate accounts are created if email already exists via email/password

id.description: User can create a new dispute pack with basic metadata
steps: Click New Dispute Pack from dashboard
Enter dispute amount
Enter response deadline date
Enter cardholder name (optional)
Enter last 4 digits of card (optional)
Enter Stripe dispute ID (optional)
Click Continue
passes: Pack record created in database with status Draft
Deadline visible on pack card in dashboard
All fields validated (amount is numeric, date is in the future)
Pack ID generated and stored
id.notes: Stripe dispute ID is optional but should be validated as a Stripe dispute ID format if provided (ch_xxx or dp_xxx).
ux_acceptance: Dispute category selector renders all 6 category options with icon and label
Each category card shows a brief description of when to use it
Selecting a category highlights it visually (border, background, or check mark)
Only one category can be selected at a time
Next button is disabled until a category is selected
Category selection persists if user navigates back to this step
Category labels use plain language — no Visa/Mastercard jargon exposed to user

id.description: User can select a dispute reason category
steps: After creating pack metadata, view category selection screen
Select one of: Fraudulent, Product Not Received, Product Not as Described, Subscription Cancelled, Duplicate Charge, Credit Not Processed
passes: Category stored on pack record
Category-specific intake form displayed immediately after selection
Category-specific evidence checklist previewed in sidebar or below form
User cannot proceed without selecting a category
id.notes: This is MAGIC MOMENT #1: category selection must immediately reveal the specific evidence checklist. This is the aha moment.
ux_acceptance: Transaction detail form renders with: amount, date, card last 4, merchant descriptor
Amount field accepts numeric input with up to 2 decimal places — rejects letters
Date field restricts to past dates only (no future dates selectable)
Card last 4 field enforces exactly 4 numeric digits
Merchant descriptor field accepts free text up to 100 characters
All required fields show inline validation errors before step advance
Partial draft saves automatically so user can return to a started pack

id.description: User answers category-specific intake questionnaire
steps: View intake questions specific to selected dispute category
Answer all required fields
Answer optional fields where applicable
Click Save and Continue
passes: All required fields validated before proceeding
Answers stored to database
Partial answers auto-saved on blur/change
User can go back and edit answers before generation
No open-ended text prompts — all fields are structured (dropdowns, checkboxes, date pickers, short inputs)
id.notes: MAGIC MOMENT #2: after answering 2-3 questions, show a partial narrative preview forming in real time. This demonstrates value before payment.
ux_acceptance: Upload area renders with clear drag-and-drop zone and 'Browse files' fallback
Accepted file types: PDF, PNG, JPEG, WEBP — others show unsupported error
Files over 10MB show inline error 'File too large — max 10MB per file'
Each uploaded file shows filename, file size, and a remove button
Multiple files can be uploaded in a single session
Upload progress indicator is visible during file transfer
Removed files are not included in the final pack

id.description: User can upload and label exhibits
steps: View exhibit upload section
Upload file (PDF, PNG, JPG accepted; max 10MB per file; max 20 files)
Enter label for each exhibit (e.g., Exhibit A: Order Confirmation)
Reorder exhibits via drag-and-drop
Delete exhibit before generation
passes: Files uploaded to Supabase Storage
File type and size validated before upload
Labels required before proceeding to generation
File list persists on page refresh
Rejected file types show clear error
id.notes: Storage uses signed URLs. Files never publicly accessible. Max 20 exhibits enforced server-side.
ux_acceptance: Pack preview renders all submitted evidence sections in final PDF order
Preview layout matches the generated PDF output accurately
Each evidence section is clearly labeled by type (e.g. 'Order Confirmation')
Back button returns to the evidence upload step without data loss
Generate Pack button is only enabled when all required evidence is present
Missing required evidence items are highlighted with a warning state

id.description: Evidence gap detection warns user of missing critical exhibits
steps: Complete intake form
View pre-generation checklist review screen
System flags any critical evidence items that are missing based on category
passes: Missing critical items shown with clear warning (not blocking — user can proceed)
Warning message explains why each item matters
User can acknowledge and proceed or go back to upload
id.notes: Gap detection is a key anti-commodity feature. Must be category-specific. Non-blocking by design — merchant may legitimately lack some evidence.
ux_acceptance: PDF generation shows an animated progress indicator during assembly
Generated PDF includes all uploaded evidence in structured order
PDF includes cover page: dispute category, transaction details, and timestamp
PDF filename follows pattern: chargeback-pack-[PACK-ID].pdf
Download button appears only after successful generation
Error state provides retry option and error description — no silent failure
Pack is associated with user account for 72-hour access window per PRD

id.description: User is gated at pack generation with a payment requirement
steps: Click Generate Pack
If no pack credits available, redirected to payment selection
Select Single Pack ($39) or 3-Pack Bundle ($99)
Click Buy Now
passes: Stripe Checkout session created server-side
User redirected to Stripe-hosted Checkout
No payment info stored in app database
Stripe publishable key used client-side only
id.notes: Payment gate must be server-side enforced. Client-side credit checks are for UX only. Server re-validates credits on generation request.
ux_acceptance: Pricing page shows $39/pack price clearly before any payment action
Checkout flow opens Stripe-hosted or embedded payment form
Order summary in checkout reads: '1 Chargeback Evidence Pack — $39'
Credit card fields use Stripe Elements with inline validation
Submit button shows loading state and disables to prevent duplicate charges
Successful payment redirects to pack creation or confirmation screen
Failed payment displays Stripe error message (e.g., 'Your card was declined')

id.description: Stripe webhook provisions pack credit after successful payment
steps: Complete payment on Stripe Checkout
Stripe fires checkout.session.completed webhook
Server verifies webhook signature with STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
Server provisions 1 or 3 pack credits to user account
User redirected to dashboard with credit available
passes: Webhook signature verified using raw body (not parsed JSON)
Idempotency key prevents duplicate credit grant on webhook retry
Credit visible in dashboard immediately after redirect
Failed webhook delivery logged and alertable
id.notes: CRITICAL: Raw body required for Stripe signature verification. Never use parsed req.body. Use stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, secret).
ux_acceptance: Confirmation screen renders immediately after successful Stripe payment
Confirmation shows: pack ID, amount paid, and user email
Transactional receipt email is sent to user's registered email address
Prominent CTA to proceed to pack creation or download is visible
Refreshing the confirmation page does not trigger a second charge
Back navigation from confirmation does not re-open payment form

id.description: Pack credit is consumed on PDF generation
steps: User has 1+ pack credits
User clicks Generate Pack (with all required fields completed)
Server atomically checks credit, deducts 1, begins generation
passes: Credit deducted before generation begins (not after)
If generation fails, credit is restored
Credit count updated in UI after generation
Double-generation prevented (idempotency on pack generation request)
id.notes: Atomic deduction prevents race condition where user triggers two simultaneous generations.
ux_acceptance: Refund policy is visible on the pricing page and in checkout flow
Refund policy page exists at /refund with content matching PRD terms
Refund request link is accessible from the account or billing settings page
Refund form requires: pack ID and reason for refund
Submission of refund request shows immediate confirmation message
Refund eligibility window matches PRD policy (no guarantees language used)

id.description: AI generates category-specific rebuttal narrative from intake answers
steps: System calls OpenAI GPT-4o with category-specific prompt and sanitized merchant answers
Narrative generated server-side
Narrative stored to database
passes: Narrative is professional, third-person dispute language
Narrative does not contain guaranteed win language
Narrative references specific intake answers (not generic boilerplate)
Narrative does not exceed 600 words
Generation completes within 30 seconds
id.notes: API key never exposed client-side. Prompt is category-specific, not open-ended. Output must be reviewed in QA for each category before launch.
ux_acceptance: AI-generated narrative section is included in the PDF for all 6 dispute categories
Narrative is category-specific — not generic boilerplate text
Narrative references the specific transaction details entered by the user
Tone is formal and professional — 'submission-grade' per brand guidelines
Narrative is under 300 words per section
No placeholder text, template tokens, or bracket-style variables appear in output
Narrative generation completes within 30 seconds or shows progress indicator

id.description: PDF assembled with correct structure and labeling
steps: After narrative generation, system assembles PDF
PDF structure: Cover page → Category checklist (checked/unchecked) → Rebuttal narrative → Labeled exhibits in order
passes: Cover page shows: merchant name, dispute amount, dispute category, response deadline, generation date
Checklist shows all category evidence items with checkmark if uploaded, empty if missing
Narrative section clearly labeled
Each exhibit labeled with merchant-provided label (Exhibit A, B, etc.)
PDF passes basic readability check (no truncated text, no overlapping elements)
PDF file size under 20MB
id.notes: MAGIC MOMENT #3: The first open of this PDF must look like a lawyer produced it. Visual quality is non-negotiable.
ux_acceptance: Each of the 6 dispute categories renders a distinct PDF template layout
Template includes category-specific evidence checklist on the cover or intro page
Required evidence items are visually distinguished from optional items
Template structure aligns with relevant Visa/Mastercard reason code requirements
Page order follows: cover → narrative → evidence exhibits → declaration
Template renders correctly for packs with 1 exhibit and packs with 10+ exhibits

id.description: Generated PDF available for download for 72 hours
steps: After generation, PDF stored in Supabase Storage
expires_at set to now + 72 hours on pack record
User sees countdown timer on pack card
User clicks Download
passes: Download returns signed URL valid for download session only
Expired packs show Expired status with option to regenerate
Expired files deleted from storage by background job
User warned at generation time that pack expires in 72 hours
id.notes: Retention policy: 72h for files, 90d for intake answers. Both purged on account deletion.
ux_acceptance: Final PDF is a single downloadable file combining all sections
PDF file size is under 10MB for typical evidence packs
PDF opens without errors in Chrome, macOS Preview, and Adobe Acrobat
PDF metadata includes app name and UTC generation timestamp
Pages are numbered sequentially starting from page 1
PDF passes basic accessibility check (text is selectable, not image-only)

id.description: Each pack shows response deadline with visual urgency
steps: View dashboard
See list of dispute packs with deadlines
passes: Deadline shown on every pack card
Packs with deadline within 7 days shown with yellow warning indicator
Packs with deadline within 48 hours shown with red urgent indicator
Expired deadlines shown with grey Missed indicator
Packs sorted by deadline proximity by default
id.notes: No email reminders in v1. Visual indicators only. Email reminders are v2.
ux_acceptance: Dispute window tracker shows days remaining prominently during pack creation
Calculation is based on the transaction date entered by the user
Color coding: green for more than 14 days, amber for 7–14 days, red for fewer than 7
Red/urgent state displays an additional CTA to proceed immediately
Deadline calculation uses the standard 120-day chargeback dispute window
Tracker is visible on the dashboard for all in-progress and completed packs

id.description: User can manually mark a pack as Submitted
steps: View pack detail page
Click Mark as Submitted
Confirm
passes: Pack status updated to Submitted
Submitted date stored
Pack no longer shows deadline urgency indicators
Status visible on dashboard
id.notes: No automatic submission integration in v1. Manual only.
ux_acceptance: Email reminder is sent automatically when 14 days remain in the dispute window
Second email reminder is sent when 7 days remain
Dashboard notification badge increments for unread deadline alerts
Notification links directly to the relevant pack
User can disable deadline reminders from account settings
Reminders are not sent for packs that have already been downloaded

id.description: No guaranteed win language appears anywhere in the product
steps: Review all UI copy, error messages, email templates, PDF output
Search for: win, guarantee, guaranteed, promise, certain, definitely, assured
passes: Zero instances of guaranteed win language found
All outcome-related language uses: strongest possible, best shot, submission-ready, complete evidence
PDF cover page includes disclaimer: This pack is prepared to support your dispute response. Card network decisions are final and cannot be guaranteed.
id.notes: This is a legal and trust requirement. Failure here is a launch blocker.
ux_acceptance: Evidence checklist renders per dispute category with all relevant item types
Each checklist item shows required or optional status clearly
Items are checked off automatically when corresponding evidence is uploaded
Progress indicator shows count of completed vs total required items
Checklist state persists across browser sessions
Incomplete required items block pack generation with a visible warning

id.description: Retention policy disclosed clearly to users
steps: View generation confirmation screen
View account settings
View privacy policy
passes: Generation screen shows: Your pack will be available to download for 72 hours. After that, your uploaded files are permanently deleted.
Account settings shows intake answer retention: Your answers are retained for 90 days to allow re-generation.
Privacy policy accurately describes retention policy
id.notes: Users uploading sensitive business documents need explicit data handling disclosure.
ux_acceptance: Pack cover page displays the mapped Visa/Mastercard reason code for the category
Reason code is shown alongside a plain-language description
Reason codes match current Visa and Mastercard dispute reason code schema
User-facing label uses plain language — raw reason code is in parentheses
Reason code mapping is reviewable before pack generation

id.description: Stripe webhook verified with signature before any action
steps: Stripe sends webhook to /api/webhooks/stripe
Server reads raw body
Server calls stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET)
passes: Invalid signatures return 400 and take no action
Valid signatures proceed to provisioning
Webhook handler does not use parsed JSON body for signature verification
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET set via environment variable, never hardcoded
id.notes: CRITICAL security requirement. Failure here means payments can be spoofed.
ux_acceptance: Declaration page appears as the final page of every generated pack
Declaration includes: merchant name, dispute category, pack ID, and generation date
Language is formal and professional — no 'guarantee' or 'win' claims per brand guidelines
Declaration is styled as a formal document section, not conversational text
Declaration is not editable by the user

id.description: Category selection immediately reveals specific evidence checklist (Magic Moment #1)
steps: User selects dispute category
Evidence checklist for that category appears without page reload
Checklist shows specific document names, not generic items
passes: Checklist visible within 300ms of category selection
Checklist items are specific to category (e.g., AVS/CVV match for Fraudulent, not generic)
User reaction: Oh, it knows exactly what I need
Checklist updates if user changes category selection
id.notes: This is the primary aha moment. If this feels slow or generic, the product feels like a template. Must feel instant and specific.
ux_acceptance: First-time users see a welcome screen after completing email confirmation
Welcome screen explains the 3-step process: describe, upload, download
Primary CTA on welcome screen is 'Create Your First Pack'
Welcome screen is suppressed for returning users who have previously logged in
Welcome screen does not display on subsequent logins

id.description: Partial narrative preview forms as user answers intake questions (Magic Moment #2)
steps: User answers first 2-3 intake questions
Partial narrative snippet appears in sidebar or below form
Snippet updates as user continues answering
passes: Preview snippet appears after question 2 is answered
Snippet uses professional dispute language with merchant's actual answers
Snippet is clearly labeled: Preview of your rebuttal narrative
Full narrative not shown (preserves generation incentive)
id.notes: This demonstrates value before payment. Critical for conversion. Can be pre-generated client-side from a template for speed — does not need to call AI.
ux_acceptance: Intake wizard renders with a visible step indicator showing current and total steps
Step 1 presents dispute category selection (all 6 options)
Step 2 presents transaction detail fields
Step 3 presents evidence upload interface
Back button on all steps returns to previous step without data loss
User answers first 2-3 intake questions before being prompted to pay
Progress is saved so users can return to an incomplete intake session

id.description: Downloaded PDF looks professionally produced (Magic Moment #3)
steps: User downloads generated PDF
Opens PDF in any standard PDF viewer
Judges visual quality against expectation
passes: Cover page has clear professional layout with all key details
Section headers are clearly formatted and labeled
Exhibits are labeled with merchant-provided labels
Font is readable (min 11pt body text)
Page numbers present
No lorem ipsum, placeholder text, or template artifacts
Reviewer impression: This looks like a lawyer produced it
id.notes: This is the moment that justifies $39 and drives word-of-mouth. Visual quality is non-negotiable. QA must review actual PDF output before launch.
ux_acceptance: FAQ page exists at /faq and renders accordion-style sections
Each accordion item expands on click and collapses on second click
FAQ covers at minimum: 'What is a chargeback?', 'How long does this take?', 'What file types are accepted?'
Help icon or link is accessible from all app screens (sidebar or header)
Clicking help from within a specific pack step links to the relevant FAQ section
FAQ page is accessible without login

id.description: SEO category landing pages exist for all 6 dispute types with full evidence requirements, structured tables, and conversion CTAs
steps: Navigate to /chargeback/fraudulent
Navigate to /chargeback/subscription-cancelled
Navigate to /chargeback/product-not-received
Navigate to /chargeback/product-not-as-described
Navigate to /chargeback/duplicate-charge
Navigate to /chargeback/credit-not-processed

Verify each page has a definition paragraph, evidence requirements table, what card networks look for, common reasons disputes are lost, narrative structure guidance, FAQ section, and a category-specific CTA
passes: All 6 category pages return HTTP 200
Each page has a unique title tag matching the formula: [Dispute Type] Chargeback Evidence Requirements | Chargeback Evidence Pack Builder
Each page has a unique meta description under 160 characters
Evidence requirements table is present and populated with dispute-specific fields
CTA button copy names the specific dispute type (not generic Get Started)
No-guarantee statement is present on each page
Price ($39) is disclosed before the CTA
No-subscription line is present near the CTA
id.notes: Six pages total. Each page serves a specific SEO keyword cluster. Evidence requirements must match Visa/Mastercard/Amex guidelines. See growth/SEO-AEO-GEO-Plan.md Section 3 for per-page specs.
ux_acceptance: Category chip for the current page is visually active/selected in the category navigation strip
Evidence requirements table renders with correct columns: Evidence Item, Required or Supplementary, Notes
CTA button is identity-framed: Build My [Dispute Type] Evidence Pack
No-guarantee statement renders as inline paragraph, not a footnote or tooltip
Price is visible above the fold or within first scroll on desktop
Page passes Core Web Vitals (LCP under 2.5s, CLS under 0.1) on mobile

id.description: FAQ schema markup (JSON-LD FAQPage) is implemented on FAQ page, pricing page, how-it-works page, and all category landing pages
steps: Navigate to /faq and inspect page source for JSON-LD FAQPage schema
Navigate to /pricing and inspect for FAQPage or HowTo schema
Navigate to /how-it-works and inspect for HowTo schema
Navigate to each of the 6 category pages and inspect for FAQPage schema
Validate schema using Google Rich Results Test
passes: JSON-LD script tag with @type FAQPage is present on /faq
Each FAQ question and answer on /faq is represented in the schema
HowTo schema is present on /how-it-works with correct steps
SoftwareApplication schema is present on homepage with name, applicationCategory, and offers fields
BreadcrumbList schema is present on all category and guide pages
Google Rich Results Test returns no errors for any page
Schema does not contain fabricated review counts or unverifiable ratings
id.notes: Schema types by page: Home = SoftwareApplication + FAQPage, How It Works = HowTo, Pricing = FAQPage, FAQ = FAQPage, Category pages = FAQPage + BreadcrumbList, Guide pages = Article + BreadcrumbList. See growth/SEO-AEO-GEO-Plan.md Section 1.5.
ux_acceptance: No visible change to page UI from schema addition
JSON-LD is injected in the document head, not inline in the body
Schema validates without errors in Google Rich Results Test
FAQ entries in schema exactly match FAQ entries visible on the page (no schema-stuffing)

id.description: Guide pages exist at /guide/* providing authority content optimised for AEO citation and long-tail SEO
steps: Navigate to /guide/chargeback-rebuttal-letter
Navigate to /guide/stripe-chargeback-response
Navigate to /guide/chargeback-evidence-requirements
Navigate to /guide/chargeback-reason-codes
Navigate to /guide/chargeback-deadlines
Verify each guide has a definition anchor, structured tables or numbered lists, FAQ section, and a contextual CTA within the first 300 words
passes: All 5 guide pages return HTTP 200
Each guide has a unique title tag and meta description
Article JSON-LD schema is present with datePublished and dateModified fields
At least one table or numbered list is present on each guide page (required for AEO citation)
A contextual CTA appears within the first 300 words of each guide
Each guide links internally to at least two category landing pages
Guide pages do not make outcome guarantees or legal claims
id.notes: Five guide pages in P1 and P2. These pages are optimised for answer engine citation: declarative fact-first copy, H2/H3 headings phrased as questions, tables not images, year-dated titles. See growth/SEO-AEO-GEO-Plan.md Section 2.
ux_acceptance: Guide pages use the same header, footer, and navigation as marketing pages
Tables render correctly on mobile (horizontal scroll or responsive layout, not overflow-hidden)
Contextual CTA within first 300 words is visually distinct from body text (button or highlighted block)
Internal links to category pages are present and functional
datePublished year appears in the page title or subtitle (e.g. 2026 Chargeback Rebuttal Letter Guide)

id.description: Structured metadata system is implemented: unique title tags, meta descriptions, and OG tags for all 15 launch pages
steps: Inspect page source of all 15 launch pages for title, meta description, og:title, og:description, og:image
Verify title tags follow the formula defined in growth/SEO-AEO-GEO-Plan.md Section 1.3
Verify meta descriptions are under 160 characters and contain a primary keyword
Verify OG image is set and resolves to a real image URL
Test with a link unfurl tool (e.g. opengraph.xyz) to confirm social sharing renders correctly
passes: All 15 pages have unique title tags (no duplicates)
All 15 pages have unique meta descriptions under 160 characters
All 15 pages have og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url
No title tag exceeds 60 characters
OG image resolves and renders in social share preview
Canonical URLs are set on all pages
No noindex meta tag is present on any intended-to-rank page
id.notes: Metadata must be server-rendered (not client-side JS injection) to be reliably crawled. In Next.js, use the Metadata API in layout.tsx and page.tsx files. See growth/SEO-AEO-GEO-Plan.md Section 1.3 for the full per-page metadata table.
ux_acceptance: Title and meta description are not visible in the page UI (head-only)
Browser tab shows the correct page title for each of the 15 pages
Social share preview (OG) renders the correct title, description, and image when URL is shared in Slack or Twitter

id.description: Pricing page implements the full CRO conversion path: anchor table, feature list, no-guarantee disclosure, and a single purchase CTA with trust signals
steps: Navigate to /pricing
Verify the comparison anchor table (consultant vs DIY vs pack builder) is present
Verify the feature list describes outcomes, not technical implementation
Verify the no-guarantee disclosure is a prominent paragraph, not a footnote
Verify the CTA button reads Build My Evidence Pack - $39
Verify the trust line below the CTA reads Secure checkout via Stripe. No subscription.
Verify the 72-hour access policy is disclosed before the purchase CTA
Verify logged-in users who already have a pack see a different state (not the full purchase flow)
passes: Comparison anchor table is visible above the fold or within first scroll on desktop
CTA button copy includes the price: Build My Evidence Pack - $39
No-guarantee disclosure appears as a full paragraph before the CTA
Secure checkout via Stripe and No subscription appear within 200px of the CTA
72-hour access policy is disclosed on the pricing page before purchase
Logged-in users with an existing pack see pack access link, not the purchase CTA
No free trial or freemium language appears anywhere on the page
id.notes: See growth/CRO-Plan.md Section 2 for full step-by-step pricing page conversion path. The anchor table (consultant $300-500 vs pack builder $39) is the highest-leverage pricing element. Do not remove it without A/B test data.
ux_acceptance: Comparison anchor table renders correctly on mobile (not overflowing viewport)
CTA button is the only primary action on the page (no competing links or secondary CTAs above it)
No-guarantee disclosure text is visually distinct from surrounding content (different background or border)
Price ($39) appears in the CTA button label, not only in surrounding text
Page does not show any subscription pricing language or trial offer UI

id.description: Trust signal system is implemented across all marketing pages with correct placement, accurate claims, and no prohibited signals
steps: Verify Stripe badge (Built for Stripe) is present on homepage, pricing page, and all category pages
Verify no-subscription statement appears within 200px of every purchase CTA sitewide
Verify no-guarantee disclosure is present on pricing page and FAQ
Verify 72-hour retention policy is disclosed on pricing page and post-purchase confirmation
Verify evidence format sourcing statement appears on category pages
Audit all pages for prohibited trust signals: win rates, guaranteed results, AI-powered as primary trust, Stripe-certified, unverifiable review counts
passes: Stripe badge is present on homepage hero, pricing page, and all 6 category pages
No-subscription statement appears near every CTA that leads to purchase
No page contains the phrases: guaranteed win, win rate, Stripe-certified, Stripe-approved, or AI-powered as a trust headline
No fabricated review counts or star ratings are present at launch
No-guarantee disclosure is present as a prominent paragraph on pricing and FAQ pages
Retention policy (72-hour access) is disclosed before purchase
id.notes: Trust architecture is detailed in growth/CRO-Plan.md Section 4. Anti-trust signals list must be enforced at code review. The no-subscription signal is the highest-anxiety trust point for this ICP (merchants who have just been hit with an unexpected chargeback).
ux_acceptance: Stripe badge renders as an image (not text) and links to Stripes partner/built-on page if applicable
No-subscription statement is plain text, not hidden in a tooltip or collapsed section
Trust signals do not add more than 200ms to page load (images are optimised, not blockers)
On mobile, trust signals remain visible and are not pushed below the fold by layout reflow

id.description: Objection handling copy is implemented inline at the exact moment each objection arises in the user journey, not only in the FAQ
steps: Homepage: verify the 3-item objection strip is present between the trust block and the final CTA
Pricing page: verify the no-guarantee objection is handled as a paragraph, not a tooltip
Builder step 1: verify the file privacy objection is handled with inline helper text
Builder step 2: verify the missing evidence objection is handled with inline helper text
Category pages: verify the failed-previous-response objection is addressed in the intro paragraph
Everywhere a price is shown: verify the one-time-payment objection is handled with the no-subscription trust line
passes: Homepage objection strip contains responses to: guarantee question, price-for-small-dispute, ChatGPT alternative
Pricing page no-guarantee paragraph is present and matches approved copy from growth/CRO-Plan.md Section 3
Builder file upload step has inline text: Files are used only to generate your pack and are not stored after your session ends
Builder evidence step has inline text confirming critical vs supplementary evidence distinction
Category page intro paragraph addresses the failed-previous-response objection
No-subscription line appears within 200px of every price mention
id.notes: 10-objection handling map is in growth/CRO-Plan.md Section 3. Each objection has a defined placement. Do not consolidate all objection handling into the FAQ page - the objection must be handled at the moment of anxiety, not on a separate page.
ux_acceptance: Objection strip on homepage is a horizontal 3-item layout on desktop, stacked vertically on mobile
Inline builder help text does not obscure form fields or block primary actions
Objection responses are 1-2 sentences maximum: scannable, not paragraph-length
Objection copy matches the approved phrasing in growth/CRO-Plan.md Section 3 exactly

id.description: All CTAs on category landing pages are identity-framed and dispute-type-specific, not generic
steps: Navigate to each of the 6 category landing pages
Verify the primary CTA button names the specific dispute type
Verify a secondary CTA (See what is included in a pack) links to the pricing page
Verify the final CTA at the bottom of the page matches the primary CTA copy
Verify no category page uses generic CTA copy: Get Started, Learn More, Try It, or Sign Up
passes: Fraudulent page CTA reads: Build My Fraudulent Dispute Evidence Pack
Subscription Cancelled page CTA reads: Build My Subscription Cancellation Evidence Pack
Product Not Received page CTA reads: Build My Product Not Received Evidence Pack
Product Not as Described page CTA reads: Build My Product Not as Described Evidence Pack
Duplicate Charge page CTA reads: Build My Duplicate Charge Evidence Pack
Credit Not Processed page CTA reads: Build My Credit Not Processed Evidence Pack
Secondary CTA See what is included in a pack ($39) is present after the evidence table
Final CTA at page bottom matches primary CTA copy exactly
id.notes: Identity-framed CTAs are the highest-leverage CTA optimisation on category pages. Generic CTAs (Get Started) on category pages are a confirmed conversion leak. See growth/CRO-Plan.md Section 8. CTA copy is fixed - do not change without A/B test data.
ux_acceptance: CTA button width accommodates the full dispute-type-specific label without truncation on mobile
Primary CTA is visually prominent: full-width on mobile, centred with clear contrast on desktop
Secondary CTA is visually subordinate to primary CTA (smaller, outlined, or text link style)
CTA copy is consistent: the exact same dispute type label appears in the hero, mid-page, and bottom CTA

id.description: Webhook events are stored with a unique event_id to guarantee idempotency — duplicate Stripe webhook deliveries cannot grant credits twice.
steps: Stripe delivers checkout.session.completed webhook to /api/webhooks/stripe
Server attempts INSERT into webhook_events table with the Stripe event_id (UNIQUE constraint)
If INSERT succeeds, proceed to grant credits via add_credits() RPC
If INSERT fails with unique violation, credits were already granted — return 200 without re-processing
Webhook returns 200 in both cases to prevent Stripe from retrying

passes.description: First delivery of checkout.session.completed grants correct credit quantity
Duplicate delivery of same event_id does not grant additional credits
Webhook returns 200 on duplicate delivery (no Stripe retry triggered)

id.notes: The webhook_events table has a UNIQUE constraint on event_id. The INSERT itself is the idempotency guard — no separate SELECT-then-INSERT pattern needed.

id.description: An hourly retention cron job deletes files from Supabase Storage for packs that have passed their 72-hour expiry window.
steps: cron-job.org calls POST /api/cron/retention with Authorization: Bearer CRON_SECRET header
Route validates CRON_SECRET — returns 401 if invalid
Query packs where status != 'expired' AND expires_at < now()
For each expired pack (batch of 100): delete all files from pack-exhibits and pack-pdfs buckets
Update pack status to 'expired' in database
Return count of packs processed

passes.description: Route rejects requests with invalid or missing CRON_SECRET
Files are deleted from both storage buckets for expired packs
Pack status is updated to 'expired' after file deletion
Non-expired packs are not affected by the retention job

id.notes: A companion daily job (POST /api/cron/purge-answers) nulls intake_answers for packs older than 90 days. Both jobs use the same CRON_SECRET validation pattern.

id.description: Credit deduction and restoration happen via SECURITY DEFINER Postgres functions — never via raw UPDATE from application code — ensuring atomic, RLS-bypassing credit operations.
steps: On generate request: server calls deduct_credit(user_id) RPC via supabaseAdmin client
deduct_credit() atomically decrements credits WHERE credits > 0 — raises exception if insufficient
If generation succeeds: credit stays deducted, pack status set to 'generated'
If generation fails: server calls restore_credit(user_id) RPC to return the credit
restore_credit() sets credit_restored = true on pack_generations to prevent double-restore
On purchase: webhook calls add_credits(user_id, quantity) RPC to grant credits

passes.description: deduct_credit() fails with error when user has 0 credits — generation is blocked
Credit is restored exactly once on generation failure (credit_restored flag prevents double restore)
add_credits() correctly increments credits on first purchase and subsequent purchases
Credits cannot go below 0 (CHECK constraint on user_credits.credits)

id.notes: SECURITY DEFINER functions bypass RLS and run as the function owner. This is intentional — credit operations must succeed regardless of the calling user's RLS context. Functions are defined with SECURITY DEFINER and owned by postgres.

id.description: PDF downloads are served via short-lived signed URLs issued by the server — never via direct storage paths — ensuring ownership validation happens on every download.
steps: User clicks download button — client calls GET /api/packs/[packId]/download
Server authenticates request via Supabase Auth JWT
Server queries pack record and verifies pack.user_id === authenticated user ID
Server generates a 15-minute signed URL for the PDF in pack-pdfs bucket via service role client
Signed URL is returned to client (never the raw storage path)
Client redirects browser to signed URL — Supabase Storage serves the file directly

passes.description: Authenticated owner can download their PDF via the signed URL
Authenticated user cannot download another user's PDF (IDOR check)
Unauthenticated request to download endpoint returns 401
Signed URL expires after 15 minutes — reuse after expiry returns 400 from Supabase Storage

id.notes: The pack-pdfs bucket has no public access. Signed URLs are the only valid download mechanism. Raw storage paths are never exposed to the client.

id.description: Server validates uploaded file type via magic bytes, not client-provided Content-Type
steps: Upload a file with .pdf extension whose content is actually plain text
Observe server reads first 8 bytes from storage after upload
Confirm server checks for PDF magic bytes: 25 50 44 46
Repeat with .png extension on a JPEG file
Confirm server checks for PNG magic bytes: 89 50 4E 47
passes: File renamed to .pdf but not a PDF is rejected with 422 Invalid file type
Legitimate PDF passes magic bytes check
Legitimate PNG passes magic bytes check
Legitimate JPEG passes magic bytes check
File with unsupported type (e.g. .docx content) is rejected
fails: Server accepts a non-PDF file that has .pdf extension
Server trusts client-provided Content-Type header without reading file bytes
Magic bytes check not performed after storage upload

id.description: Application sends Content Security Policy and security headers on all responses
steps: Load any page and inspect response headers in browser DevTools Network tab
Verify Content-Security-Policy header is present
Verify default-src is 'none'
Verify Strict-Transport-Security header is present
Verify X-Frame-Options: DENY is present
Verify X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff is present
passes: CSP header present with default-src 'none'
script-src allows only 'self', js.stripe.com, browser.sentry-cdn.com
HSTS header present with max-age >= 31536000
X-Frame-Options: DENY present
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff present
No unsafe-eval in CSP
fails: CSP header absent or set to default-src *

id.description: Rate limiting enforced on auth and generation endpoints
steps: Send 11 POST requests to /api/auth/login from the same IP within 15 minutes
Observe response on the 11th request
Authenticate as a user and send 6 POST requests to /api/packs/[id]/generate within 1 hour
Observe response on the 6th request
passes: 11th auth request from same IP within 15 min returns 429 Too Many Requests
6th generation request within 1 hour for same user returns 429
Rate limit resets after the window expires
Requests within limit succeed normally
fails: More than 10 auth requests succeed from same IP within 15 minutes
More than 5 generation requests succeed for same user within 1 hour

id.description: Intake form answers are sanitized before being rendered in Puppeteer PDF generation
steps: Submit a pack with an intake answer containing <script>alert(1)</script>
Trigger PDF generation
Inspect generated PDF for script execution evidence
Submit intake answer containing an image tag with external URL
Verify Puppeteer request interception blocks the external fetch
passes: Script tags in intake answers do not execute during PDF render
HTML entities in intake answers are escaped before insertion into PDF template
External URLs in intake content are blocked by request interception
fails: Script tag in intake answer executes JavaScript during PDF render
External URL in intake answer causes Puppeteer to make a network request
Raw HTML from intake answer appears unescaped in PDF output

id.description: Puppeteer request interception blocks all non-data-URI requests during PDF render
steps: Craft an intake answer containing a reference to an external URL (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/)
Trigger PDF generation with this intake answer
Monitor network traffic during generation
Inspect Puppeteer logs for aborted requests
passes: All non-data: URI requests are aborted during PDF render
PDF generation completes successfully with data: URIs allowed
No outbound HTTP requests made by Puppeteer to external hosts
Internal cloud metadata endpoint 169.254.169.254 is unreachable from Puppeteer
fails: Puppeteer makes HTTP request to an external URL during PDF render
Puppeteer fetches from 169.254.169.254 or equivalent metadata endpoint

id.description: Supabase auth tokens stored in HttpOnly cookies, not localStorage
steps: Log in to the application
Open browser DevTools → Application → Local Storage → app domain
Check for keys with sb- prefix
Open DevTools → Application → Cookies → app domain
Inspect auth-related cookies for HttpOnly flag
passes: No sb- prefixed keys present in localStorage after login
Auth cookies have HttpOnly flag set
Auth cookies have Secure flag set
Auth cookies have SameSite=Strict attribute
fails: sb-access-token or sb-refresh-token found in localStorage
Tokens accessible via document.cookie in browser console

id.description: Row Level Security is enabled and verified on all public Supabase tables after migrations
steps: Run migration against a clean database
Execute SQL: SELECT tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public' AND rowsecurity=false
Authenticate as User A and attempt to query User B's pack via direct Supabase client call
Verify RLS policy blocks cross-user data access
passes: SQL query returns 0 rows (all tables have RLS enabled)
User A cannot read User B's packs or exhibits via direct Supabase client
All public tables have at least one RLS policy defined
Service role bypasses RLS only when explicitly using service role client
fails: Any table returned by the RLS-disabled SQL query
User A can read or modify User B's data via Supabase client
RLS enabled but no policies defined (blocks all access)

id.description: API routes return only internal_error on unhandled exceptions, with no stack trace or internal detail
steps: Trigger an intentional server error (e.g. pass invalid parameter to a route)
Inspect the response body
Inspect response headers for server version or framework info
Verify Sentry received the full error details
passes: Response body is exactly {"error":"internal_error"} on 500
No stack trace, function name, or file path in response
No server version or framework headers exposed
Sentry dashboard shows full error with stack trace
fails: Error message or stack trace visible in API response body
Error includes database column names or internal function names
Exception propagates to client with raw JavaScript error

id.description: Audit log events emitted for credit operations, auth events, uploads, and webhook processing
steps: Log in and observe stdout logs for auth.login event
Upload an exhibit file and observe upload.success or upload.rejected event
Trigger a credit deduction and observe credits.deducted event
Send a duplicate webhook event and observe webhook.duplicate event
Verify each log entry includes event, user_id, timestamp, and metadata fields
passes: auth.login event emitted on successful login with user_id
upload.success event emitted with file metadata
upload.rejected event emitted with rejection reason
credits.deducted event emitted with pack_id and credit amount
webhook.duplicate event emitted when event_id already processed
Each log entry is valid JSON with required fields
fails: No log emitted on credit deduction
No log emitted on rejected upload
Log entries missing user_id or timestamp
Duplicate webhook processed without webhook.duplicate log entry

id.description: Uploaded files stored under UUID keys; original filenames sanitized and stored only in database
steps: Upload a file with a filename containing path traversal characters (e.g. ../../etc/passwd.pdf)
Observe the storage key assigned to the file in Supabase Storage
Check pack_exhibits table for the original_name column value
Verify the storage path does not contain the original filename
passes: Storage key is a UUID (e.g. 3f2a1b4c-...) with no relation to original filename
Original filename with traversal characters is stored sanitized in original_name column
File is accessible only via signed URL based on UUID key
Filename containing ../ does not create unexpected storage paths
fails: Storage key includes original filename or path-traversal characters
File stored at a path that could be predicted from the original filename
No sanitization applied to original_name before database storage
